channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Meta Is in Crisis, Google Search’s Makeover, and AI Gets Booed by Graduates
item.description: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we unpack the mass layoffs at Meta, big announcements at Google I/O, and the latest backlash against AI.

item.title: 'A true engineering feat': Silicon-based qubits have a clear advantage in race to million-qubit quantum computer thanks to tech that made Nvidia AI GPUs possible
item.description: Quantum computing using silicon-based qubits has a unique set of advantages versus other approaches

item.title: Best Window Air Conditioners of 2026: Midea, Zafro, GE
item.description: These are the AC units we’ve trusted to cool our homes for months, if not years.

item.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s battle over OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. After nearly a month, […]

item.title: Firefox is working on a rounded redesign with easy-to-find controls for privacy and AI
item.description: Firefox is getting a drastic visual overhaul with a redesigned Settings section that will make it easier to find and use privacy settings, including the switch for turning off all present and future AI features. Mozilla calls the redesign "Project Nova" and plans to begin rolling it out later this year. It features rounded UI […]

item.title: In desperate times, graduates find hope in humiliating tech CEOs
item.description: University graduates are booing and heckling corporate executives who praise AI during their commencement ceremonies, and the only people who seem to be genuinely surprised by this are the executives themselves. In a procession of viral videos, 2026 commencement speakers like former Google CEO Eric Schmidt face loud and sustained jeers from students after praising […]...

item.title: The biggest surprise from Google I/O 2026? Poll reveals majority of Android fans are most excited for Android XR and Gemini Spark— but we’re still waiting to hear more about Googlebooks and the Google Smart speaker
item.description: Poll reveals most Google I/O announcements had been what Android fans wanted, but there was nothing about the next Google Home speaker and Googlebooks.

item.title: Who gets to own the Luigi Mangione story?
item.description: On Monday morning, a judge overseeing the New York state case on the killing of the UnitedHealthcare CEO ruled that some evidence collected by police could not be shown to a jury. It wasn't the only news coming out of the hearing. Outside the courthouse, Molly Crane-Newman, a New York Daily News reporter, captured on […]

item.title: Spotify and Universal Music strike deal allowing fan-made AI covers and remixes
item.description: Spotify is partnering with Universal Music Group￼ to let Premium subscribers create AI-generated song covers and remixes, with participating artists receiving a share of the revenue.

item.title: Philips’ new display has a screen on both sides
item.description: Its name might be dull and uninspired, but the Philips 24B2D5300 Business Monitor brings a novel feature I've never seen on a display before: screens on either side. The design will primarily benefit people who are constantly angling their computer screen so those on both sides of a desk can see it, like a car […]

item.title: Forget ‘TechnoKing’: Elon Musk will really be king at SpaceX
item.description: Musk, who will have more than 50% of the voting power, will have a monarchical grip over the publicly-traded version of SpaceX — control that goes far beyond what other tech founders enjoy.

item.title: Law enforcement shuts down VPN service used by two dozen ransomware gangs
item.description: First VPN promised hackers complete anonymity for their cyberattacks. But Europol said it was able to notify the service’s users that they have now been identified.

item.title: The U.S. still doesn’t have a surgeon general — so RFK Jr. just stepped in with a controversial warning about kids and screens
item.description: The Surgeon General’s office says children are spending so much time on screens that the issue has become a growing public health concern. The U.S. may not have a surgeon general right now, but that isn’t stopping Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. from sending out a public health advisory usually reserved for America’s top doctor.

item.title: Bungie will end active development of Destiny 2 on June 9
item.description: Destiny 2's active development will end on June 9.

item.title: Six search engines worth trying now that Google isn’t really Google anymore
item.description: Google is about to look really different, and if you're not a fan of the AI overview feature, then you're not going to like what's coming.

item.title: I found a lot of cheap Memorial Day business laptops on Amazon — here are the only ones worth buying under $600 from Dell, Lenovo, and HP
item.description: Don't be fooled by all those ultra-cheap business laptop deals in Amazon's sale - these are the ones to look for.

item.title: Californias Gavin Newsom tries to save workers from AI with executive order
item.description: California's governor wants an AI jobs loss plan for workers.

item.title: L’Antarctique cache une archive inattendue de notre voyage à travers la Galaxie et de catastrophes cosmiques récente
item.description: Les strates de roches et de glace sur Terre conservent des traces d'événements critiques de l'histoire de notre Planète bleue. On a compris aussi qu'elles contenaient la mémoire d'événements survenus non seulement dans le Système solaire, mais aussi dans notre Voie lactée.

item.title: A 0.12% parameter add-on gives AI agents the working memory RAG can't
item.description: AI agents forget. Every time a coding assistant loses track of a debugging thread, or a data analysis agent re-ingests the same context it already processed, the team pays in latency, token costs, and brittle workflows. The fix most teams reach for — expanding the context window or adding more RAG — is increasingly expensive and still doesn't reliably work.To address this, researchers from Mind Lab and several universities proposed delta-mem, an efficient technique that compresses the model’s historical information into a dynamically updated matrix without changing the model itself. The result...
